question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted StreamFile<?> with a null or empty previousHash value to exploit the SHA_384.isHashEmpty() bypass in Downloader.verifyHashChain(), causing the hash chain integrity check to be silently skipped and a tampered stream file to be accepted as verified, permanently corrupting the mirror node's view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the first-block bypass in BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain() where cutoverService.getLastRecordFile() returns empty (last == null), causing the entire hash chain verification to be skipped for the first block, allowing a tampered block with arbitrary previousHash to be passed to streamFileNotifier.verified() and ingested as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileNotifier's sequential iteration over notifiers such that if the first notifier's verified() call throws an exception, subsequent notifiers never receive the stream file, causing inconsistent state across downstream parsers and enabling a partial history manipulation where some components accept a tampered file while others do not?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the BatchStreamFileNotifier's internal ArrayBlockingQueue by submitting a high volume of stream files faster than the StreamFileSubscriber thread can consume them, causing queue.put() to block indefinitely, halting the entire verified() pipeline and preventing legitimate Hashgraph history from being ingested while the attacker's files remain queued?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFile<?> with a StreamType of BLOCK (not BALANCE or RECORD) and pass it to BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified(), triggering an IllegalArgumentException that propagates up through the notifier chain, causing the importer to crash or skip subsequent legitimate files and creating a gap in the recorded Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition in Downloader.lastStreamFile (AtomicReference) where two concurrent download threads both read the same lastStreamFile value before either updates it, causing both to pass the same expectedPrevHash into verifyHashChain(), allowing a tampered file with a replayed previousHash to bypass the chain check and be forwarded to streamFileNotifier.verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the stream file source such that a StreamFile<?> with getType().isChained() returning false is passed to Downloader.verifyHashChain(), causing the entire hash chain verification to be unconditionally skipped (returns true immediately), allowing a file with completely fabricated transaction history to be accepted and forwarded to streamFileNotifier.verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier cutover path where blockFile.hasRecordFile() is false and last.getVersion() < BlockStreamReader.VERSION, causing blockFile.setPreviousHash(previousHash) to overwrite the block's own previousHash with the record file's hash without verifying the block's original previousHash, effectively bypassing the block-level hash chain check before streamFileNotifier.verified() is called?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a wrapped record block (blockFile.hasRecordFile() == true) where recordFile.getPreviousWrappedRecordBlockHash() is null, exploiting the null check in BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain() that skips the wrapped record block hash chain verification when previousWrappedRecordBlockHash is null, allowing a tampered wrapped block to pass verification and reach streamFileNotifier.verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.setStreamFileIndex() logic where the index is derived from lastStreamFile or importerProperties.getStartBlockNumber() rather than from the stream file's own content, causing a tampered stream file with a manipulated index to be assigned an incorrect sequential index and forwarded to streamFileNotifier.verified(), corrupting the block index sequence in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.handle() buffer flush logic by timing stream file submissions to prevent the flush() condition from being met (maxFiles, maxItems, flushInterval not reached), causing verified stream files to remain buffered indefinitely and never be parsed, creating a denial-of-history scenario where recent Hashgraph transactions are never persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error recovery behavior in BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.run() where exceptions during parsing are caught and logged but execution continues, causing a tampered stream file that triggers a parse error to be silently dropped while the subscriber continues processing subsequent files, creating an undetected gap in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.onVerified() method's call to streamFile.copy().clear() which nullifies bytes and items in the cached lastStreamFile, such that if the copy() implementation is shallow, the original streamFile's hash fields are also cleared, causing subsequent hash chain verification to use a null/empty expectedPrevHash and bypass the chain check for the next file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.updateLedger() method which only updates the TSS ledger when blockFile.getIndex() == 0, by injecting a crafted block with index 0 containing a malicious LedgerIdPublicationTransaction, causing tssVerifier.setLedger() to be called with attacker-controlled ledger data that then validates all subsequent tampered block signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.verifyBlockNumber() filename parsing logic where the block number is extracted from the filename string using Long.parseLong(), by supplying a block file whose filename encodes a different block number than its content's blockFile.getIndex(), causing an InvalidStreamFileException that prevents legitimate blocks from being verified while the attacker's manipulated block number is accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-consecutive block number check in BlockStreamVerifier.verifyBlockNumber() where the check only fires when cutoverService.getLastRecordFile() is non-empty, by triggering a state where getLastRecordFile() returns empty (e.g., after a restart), allowing a block with an arbitrary non-consecutive block number to bypass the sequence check and be forwarded to streamFileNotifier.verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.verifySignatures() loop which iterates over CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures and returns true on the first successful verification, by crafting a scenario where the first CONSENSUS_REACHED signature corresponds to a tampered data file that passes hash checks due to a hash collision or empty-hash bypass, causing streamFileNotifier.verified() to be called with the tampered file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.verifyTssSignature() path where blockProof.hasSignedBlockProof() is false and blockProof.hasBlockStateProof() is false, causing an InvalidStreamFileException to be thrown before streamFileNotifier.verified() is called, and by repeatedly triggering this condition, prevent any blocks from being verified and create a complete denial of Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockStreamVerifier.getRootHash() method where if blockProof.hasSignedBlockProof() is true the raw hash is used directly without Merkle path verification, by crafting a block with a SignedBlockProof that contains a valid TSS signature over a manipulated rawHash, bypassing the state proof path validation and causing streamFileNotifier.verified() to accept a block with falsified transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.verifyHashChain() method's use of String.contentEquals() for hash comparison, by supplying a StreamFile<?> whose getPreviousHash() returns a CharSequence implementation that always returns true from contentEquals(), bypassing the hash chain check and allowing a tampered file to be forwarded to streamFileNotifier.verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Downloader.onVerified() method's metric recording using streamFile.getConsensusStart() and streamFile.getConsensusEnd() without re-validating these values after hash verification, by supplying a stream file with manipulated consensusStart/consensusEnd timestamps that pass hash checks but record false timing data, corrupting the mirror node's latency metrics and potentially masking history manipulation?
